prompt: Change-region restriction (T2060) default mode: should a finding survive if its span is strictly INSIDE a changed hunk, or if it merely OVERLAPS/touches a changed hunk? 'touching' is safer against hiding real regressions whose symptom lands just outside the edited lines; 'strict' is quieter on brushed files.
choices: touching (overlap) — recommended default, checks may opt into strict
strict (contained) — quietest, checks may opt into touching

prompt: Ship the typed fix_data channel (FixHandle + Fixer, check emits opaque typed data, a registered fixer computes edits) in v1 — or defer it to a second phase and ship only the existing precomputed-edits (SuggestedFix) path plus the change-region gate first? Yes = include typed fix_data in v1; No = defer (the doc's recommendation).

Which format should be the single v1 reference typed-projection (proof of the generic->typed graduation path)? proto is the doc's pick (semantic wire-compat is the clearest bounded case); the config-record family (yaml/json/toml) is the alternative because one small model serves three formats.
choices: both proto and config-record in v1 — more scope
